trade.summary: Bilateral trade has effectively collapsed following Australia's comprehensive sanctions imposed after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. Australia imposed multiple rounds of sanctions on Russian individuals, entities, and sectors. Russian exports of fertilisers and some commodities to Australia have been disrupted. Trade is negligible.
disputes: Comprehensive Australian sanctions in response to Ukraine invasion (2022-ongoing)
MH17 accountability — Australia jointly sued Russia at ICAO (2022)

diplomatic.summary: Australia has been among the most vocal and active Western critics of Russian aggression in Ukraine. Diplomatic relations are at an extreme low: Australia has imposed multiple rounds of sanctions, expelled Russian diplomats, provided military and humanitarian aid to Ukraine (AUD 1.3 billion+ by 2025), and actively pursued international legal accountability for Russia including MH17 and war crimes referrals to the ICC. Russia has reciprocally sanctioned Australian officials and entities.
recent_incidents: Australian military aid packages to Ukraine (2022-2025)

military.summary: No cooperation; effectively adversarial. Australia has provided substantial military support to Ukraine including Bushmasters, tanks, artillery, and training. Russian naval vessels have been tracked near Australian territorial waters. Australia participates in Western intelligence sharing targeting Russian military activities. All military-to-military contacts suspended.
incidents: Russian naval vessels transiting near Australian waters (2023)
Alleged Russian cyber operations targeting Australian government (ongoing)

financial.summary: All significant financial links severed by Australian sanctions. SWIFT exclusion of Russian banks prevents most transactions. Australian superannuation funds divested Russian assets. Russian oligarch assets in Australia subject to freezing orders. No meaningful financial relationship.

energy.summary: Australia does not import Russian energy. Before sanctions, some Russian fertiliser (energy-adjacent) reached Australian agriculture. Australia's LNG exports actively displace Russian gas in European and Asian markets, directly harming Russian energy revenues.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation effectively suspended. Australian universities have severed partnerships with Russian institutions. Russian academics banned from Australian government-funded research programs. Some individual scientific contacts continue in non-sensitive fields but institutional cooperation has ended.
